message:
fix(core): gate writeback barrier invariant types behind #[cfg(test)]

The WritebackBarrierModel, WritebackOpClass, WritebackScheduleEvent,
WritebackInvariantViolationKind, WritebackInvariantViolation types and
writeback_schedule_invariant_violations function are currently only
exercised by tests. Gate them with #[cfg(test)] so the library target
compiles without dead_code warnings.

Update bd-m5wf.2.1 to blocked (rch SSH failures) and bd-m5wf.3.1 to
in_progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","title":"Verification Gate: performance optimization (profiling + regression guards)","description":"## Purpose

Verify all performance optimization sub-beads have met their acceptance criteria with profiling evidence, behavioral equivalence proofs, and regression guards.

## Scope

1. All profiling baselines recorded and reproducible
2. Each optimization has before/after criterion comparison
3. All behavioral equivalence golden tests passing
4. No performance regressions in unrelated benchmarks
5. Structured logging for all new metrics
6. E2E script running full benchmark suite with pass/fail thresholds

## Acceptance criteria
- All sub-bead E2E scripts pass
- All sub-bead unit tests pass
- Criterion regression detection shows no regressions
- Flamegraph evidence for all optimized paths
- Summary report: operation, before, after, improvement %","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:54.704845253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:33.719075944Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarking","performance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:54.704845253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:29.163604407Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:30.256388988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:31.455563445Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:32.539409732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:33.718120302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.10","title":"Program Gate: V1.2 end-to-end acceptance (all epics + cross-cutting validation)","description":"## Purpose

Final V1.2 program acceptance gate. Validates all 9 epic verification gates have passed and cross-cutting concerns are operationally sound.

## Scope

### Scenarios

1. All 9 epic verification gate E2E scripts exist and pass
2. Workspace builds clean (fmt + clippy + 0 warnings)
3. Workspace tests pass (all crates, 0 failures)
4. Performance optimization gate (Epic 1) — profiling baselines and regression guards
5. FUSE writeback-cache gate (Epic 2) — correctness proofs and performance gains
6. Safe-merge gate (Epic 3) — correctness under high contention
7. Adaptive repair gate (Epic 4) — expected-loss improvement
8. Multi-host repair gate (Epic 5) — distributed repair correctness
9. btrfs expansion gate (Epic 6) — multi-device and subvolume correctness
10. xfstests gate (Epic 7) — conformance baseline with regression detection
11. Observability gate (Epic 8) — metrics, TUI, evidence presets
12. Test coverage gate (Epic 9) — test count targets met
13. CLI ergonomics and error messaging (all new commands)
14. Structured logging traceability across all new surfaces

### E2E script

`scripts/e2e/ffs_v12_program_gate_e2e.sh` — 14 scenarios, each producing `SCENARIO_RESULT` markers.

## Acceptance criteria
- 14/14 scenarios PASS
- All epic verification gates pass
- Workspace clean
- RELEASE RECOMMENDATION: PROCEED","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:14.984393625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:58.727785714Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:14.984393625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:49.223564252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:50.395471520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:51.564604738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:52.799485483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:53.959347624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:55.112597761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:56.286694803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:57.465102576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:58.726633224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.2","title":"EPIC: FUSE Writeback-Cache Support with MVCC Boundary Correctness","description":"## Purpose

Enable FUSE writeback-cache mode for dramatically improved write performance. Currently disabled (V1.1 OQ4 resolution: explicit flush vs fsync contract with writeback-cache disabled). Requires proving MVCC boundary correctness under reordered daemon delivery.

## Why

The FUSE writeback cache can batch and reorder writes before delivering them to the filesystem, which can break MVCC commit ordering assumptions. V1.1 conservatively disabled it. V1.2 should enable it with correctness proofs.

## Scope

### Sub-beads

1. **Design: writeback-cache MVCC interaction model** — Formal analysis of write reordering effects on MVCC snapshot visibility, commit ordering, and crash consistency. Define the invariants that must hold. Expected-loss decision matrix for enabling writeback-cache.

2. **Implement writeback-cache commit barrier** — The mechanism that ensures MVCC commit boundaries are respected despite FUSE daemon write reordering. Likely: epoch-based commit barriers, deferred visibility until flush/fsync confirmation.

3. **Crash consistency under writeback-cache** — Prove that crash at any point during writeback-cached operation recovers to a consistent state. Crash matrix with writeback-cache enabled.

4. **Performance validation** — Measure write throughput improvement with writeback-cache enabled. Target: >2x improvement on small-write workloads.

5. **Verification gate** — Correctness proofs, crash matrix, performance comparison, E2E validation.

## Non-goals
- Read caching changes (already handled by kernel page cache)
- Changing existing non-writeback-cache behavior","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:35.038010152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:35.038010152Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","design","fuse","mvcc","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:35.038010152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-m5wf.2.1","title":"Design writeback-cache MVCC interaction model and invariants","description":"## Purpose

Formally analyze how FUSE writeback-cache write reordering interacts with MVCC snapshot visibility and commit ordering. Define invariants.

## Scope

1. **Write reordering model** — Document all ways FUSE daemon can reorder writes when writeback-cache is enabled: batching, merging, delayed delivery, out-of-order delivery.

2. **MVCC invariant analysis** — For each reordering scenario, prove whether snapshot isolation is maintained. Identify which reorderings are safe and which violate MVCC semantics.

3. **Commit barrier design** — Design the mechanism (epoch barriers, flush synchronization, deferred visibility) that preserves MVCC correctness under writeback-cache.

4. **Expected-loss decision matrix** — Quantify: probability of data loss under each design option × severity. Select the design with lowest expected loss.

5. **Design document** — docs/design-writeback-cache-mvcc.md with formal invariants, proofs, and decision matrix.

## Acceptance criteria
- Design document with formal invariants
- Expected-loss decision matrix
- All reordering scenarios enumerated and analyzed
- Commit barrier mechanism specified
- Unit tests for invariant checker","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:35.872006500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:42:42.549091Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","design","fuse","mvcc","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:35.872006500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Unit tests for invariant checker","notes":"2026-03-14: Verified existing writeback-cache MVCC design doc and invariant tests. Scoped invariant-model types/functions in crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs behind #[cfg(test)] to avoid library-target dead_code warnings. Validation via rch remains blocked: fresh daemon (uptime reset) still reports RCH-E100 SSH failures on all workers even though direct ssh to vmi1153651 succeeds. cargo fmt --check succeeded; cargo check/test via rch could not be completed without local fallback.","status":"blocked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:35.872006500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T01:00:09.248052Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","design","fuse","mvcc","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:35.872006500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.2.2","title":"Implement writeback-cache commit barrier mechanism","description":"## Purpose

Implement the commit barrier mechanism that preserves MVCC ordering under FUSE writeback-cache reordering.

## Scope

1. **Epoch-based commit barriers** in ffs-fuse — Mark MVCC commit boundaries as epoch transitions. Writes within an epoch can be reordered freely; epoch boundaries are flush points.

2. **Deferred visibility** — Writes delivered out of order are not visible to other snapshots until the epoch commits.

3. **Flush synchronization** — fsync/fsyncdir triggers epoch commit, making all buffered writes visible and durable.

4. **Configuration** — Feature-gated: `--writeback-cache` CLI flag enables the mode. Default remains disabled for safety.

## Acceptance criteria
- Commit barrier implementation with epoch tracking
- Deferred visibility for out-of-order writes
- Flush triggers epoch commit
- CLI flag for opt-in enablement
- Unit tests: epoch transitions, deferred visibility, flush semantics
- E2E script: writeback-cache mode mount, write, fsync, verify","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:36.711193200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:58.695079046Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","fuse","mvcc","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:36.711193200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:58.694174110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.2.3","title":"Prove crash consistency under writeback-cache operation","description":"## Purpose

Prove that crash at any point during writeback-cached operation recovers to a consistent MVCC state.

## Scope

1. **Crash matrix** — 10+ crash points covering:
   - Crash during buffered write (before epoch commit)
   - Crash during epoch flush
   - Crash after fsync but before writeback-cache drain
   - Crash during concurrent epochs
   - Crash with partially-committed epoch

2. **Recovery verification** — After each crash point: WAL replay, verify MVCC state consistency, verify no partial epochs visible, verify fsync'd data durable.

3. **Comparison** — Same crash matrix with writeback-cache disabled, verify identical recovery outcomes.

## Acceptance criteria
- 10+ crash point scenarios
- Recovery verification for each crash point
- No partial epoch data visible after crash
- fsync'd data survives all crash points
- E2E crash matrix script with structured logging
- Unit tests for crash recovery logic","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:37.582917859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:59.767777648Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","fuse","mvcc","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:37.582917859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:59.766789526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.2.4","title":"Measure writeback-cache performance improvement","description":"## Purpose

Quantify the write throughput improvement from enabling FUSE writeback-cache.

## Scope

1. **Benchmark workloads** — Compare writeback-cache enabled vs disabled:
   - 1000 small writes (4KB each)
   - Sequential write (100MB)
   - Random write (10000 × 4KB)
   - Mixed read+write (80/20)

2. **Latency distribution** — p50/p95/p99 write latency for each workload.

3. **Criterion integration** — Benchmarks as criterion groups for regression detection.

## Acceptance criteria
- Before/after throughput comparison for 4 workloads
- >2x improvement target for small-write workload
- Latency distribution recorded
- Criterion benchmarks integrated
- E2E script with structured reporting","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:38.471291986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:10:16.640715738Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarking","fuse","performance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:38.471291986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:00.818604764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.2.5","title":"Verification Gate: writeback-cache correctness and performance","description":"## Purpose

Verify writeback-cache implementation meets all correctness and performance acceptance criteria.

## Scope

1. All crash matrix scenarios pass
2. MVCC snapshot isolation preserved under writeback-cache
3. Performance improvement >2x for small writes
4. No regressions with writeback-cache disabled
5. Design document reviewed and complete
6. All unit tests and E2E scripts pass

## Acceptance criteria
- All sub-bead E2E scripts pass
- All sub-bead unit tests pass
- Crash matrix: 10+ scenarios all pass
- Performance: documented improvement
- Design doc complete with invariants and proofs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:39.365639341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:03.232057269Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","fuse","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:39.365639341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:02.168967057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:03.231098181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.3","title":"EPIC: Safe-Merge and Adaptive Conflict Arbitration","description":"## Purpose

Implement semantic safe-merge and adaptive conflict arbitration for MVCC. Deferred from V1.1 OQ2: strict FCW+SSI was chosen as the conservative V1.x policy. V1.2 adds per-block semantic merge proofs for operations that can safely merge without data corruption.

## Why

Strict FCW+SSI aborts all conflicting transactions, even when the conflicts are semantically safe (e.g., two writers appending to different parts of a file, or two writers updating different xattrs on the same inode). Safe-merge reduces unnecessary aborts → higher throughput under concurrent workloads.

## Scope

### Sub-beads

1. **Design: safe-merge taxonomy and merge proof obligations** — Classify all block-level operations into merge-safe vs merge-unsafe categories. Define formal merge proof obligations for each safe category.

2. **Implement per-block merge proofs** — Add merge-proof annotations to MVCC write operations. Operations with valid merge proofs bypass FCW abort.

3. **Implement adaptive conflict policy** — Runtime-tunable conflict policy that selects between strict FCW, safe-merge, and future arbitration strategies based on observed contention patterns and expected-loss calculation.

4. **Contention evidence and observability** — Structured logging for conflict decisions, merge proof outcomes, and contention statistics. Live metrics for conflict rate, merge success rate, abort rate.

5. **Verification gate** — Correctness proofs, stress tests under high contention, expected-loss comparison vs strict FCW.

## Non-goals
- Distributed transaction coordination
- Cross-image merge","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:20:31.959152244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:20:31.959152244Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","correctness","design","mvcc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:20:31.959152244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-m5wf.3.1","title":"Design safe-merge taxonomy and per-block merge proof obligations","description":"## Purpose

Classify all block-level MVCC operations into merge-safe and merge-unsafe categories. Define formal proof obligations for each merge-safe category.

## Scope

1. **Operation taxonomy** — For each write operation type (data block write, inode metadata update, directory entry add/remove, xattr set/remove, bitmap allocation, extent tree modification):
   - Can two concurrent instances of this operation merge without corruption?
   - Under what conditions?
   - What invariants must hold?

2. **Merge-safe categories** (expected):
   - Disjoint data block writes (different logical blocks)
   - Append-only directory entry additions (different names)
   - Independent xattr modifications (different keys)
   - Non-overlapping extent tree modifications

3. **Merge-unsafe categories** (expected):
   - Same data block writes (last-writer-wins is not always correct)
   - Directory entry rename (atomic swap semantics)
   - Bitmap modifications (free space accounting must be exact)
   - Inode size/timestamp updates (depends on operation semantics)

4. **Formal proof obligations** — For each merge-safe category: preconditions, postconditions, invariant preservation proof sketch.

5. **Design document** — docs/design-safe-merge-taxonomy.md

## Acceptance criteria
- Complete operation taxonomy (all write types classified)
- Formal merge proof obligations for each safe category
- Design document with proof sketches
- Unit tests for merge-safety classification logic","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:09.004430437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:10:10.736674868Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","correctness","design","mvcc","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:09.004430437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-m5wf.3.2","title":"Implement per-block merge proofs in MVCC write path","description":"## Purpose

Add merge-proof annotations to MVCC write operations so that semantically safe conflicts can merge instead of aborting.

## Scope

1. **MergeProof type** — New type in ffs-mvcc:
   - `MergeProof::DisjointBlocks` — writes target different logical blocks
   - `MergeProof::AppendOnly` — both writes are append operations
   - `MergeProof::IndependentKeys` — xattr writes to different keys
   - `MergeProof::Unsafe` — no merge proof available, fall back to FCW

2. **Merge validation** — During FCW conflict detection, check if conflicting writes both carry valid merge proofs. If so, allow both to commit.

3. **Merge application** — For mergeable conflicts, combine both write sets into the committed version. Verify merged state satisfies all postconditions.

4. **Fallback** — Any write without a merge proof, or with an invalid proof, uses strict FCW (abort on conflict).

## Acceptance criteria
- MergeProof type with 4+ variants
- FCW conflict path checks merge proofs
- Merge application produces correct combined state
- Stress test: 100 concurrent writers with mergeable conflicts (0 unnecessary aborts)
- Stress test: 100 concurrent writers with unmergeable conflicts (correct aborts)
- Unit tests for each merge proof variant
- E2E script with structured logging","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:09.931138719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:04.290106061Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","correctness","mvcc","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:09.931138719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:04.289155309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.3.3","title":"Implement adaptive conflict policy with expected-loss selection","description":"## Purpose

Implement a runtime-tunable conflict policy that selects between strict FCW and safe-merge based on observed contention patterns and expected-loss calculation.

## Scope

1. **ConflictPolicy enum** — `Strict` (FCW only), `SafeMerge` (merge when proof available), `Adaptive` (auto-select based on contention).

2. **Adaptive selection** — Track:
   - Conflict rate (conflicts per commit)
   - Merge success rate (successful merges per conflict)
   - Abort rate (aborts per commit)
   - Use exponential moving average over configurable window

3. **Expected-loss calculation** — For each policy:
   - P(corruption) × severity + P(unnecessary_abort) × cost
   - Safe-merge has higher P(corruption) but lower P(unnecessary_abort)
   - Policy with lowest expected loss wins

4. **Configuration** — CLI flag `--conflict-policy strict|safe-merge|adaptive`

## Acceptance criteria
- ConflictPolicy enum with 3 variants
- Adaptive policy tracks contention metrics
- Expected-loss calculation correct
- Policy switches between strict and safe-merge based on contention
- Unit tests for expected-loss calculation
- Unit tests for policy selection logic
- E2E script demonstrating adaptive switching","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:10.854617953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:05.358232773Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bayesian","concurrency","design","mvcc","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:10.854617953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:05.357252235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.3.4","title":"Add contention evidence logging and conflict observability","description":"## Purpose

Structured logging for safe-merge-specific decisions: merge proof validation, merge application events, adaptive policy switching. Generic MVCC metrics (commit rate, conflict rate, abort rate) are handled by bd-m5wf.8.3 (Epic 8 observability). This bead focuses on safe-merge-specific tracing.

## Scope

1. **Merge-specific log markers**:
   - `mvcc_merge_proof_checked` — proof variant, valid/invalid, reason, involved block ranges
   - `mvcc_merge_applied` — merged block count, combined write set size, merge proof variant used
   - `mvcc_merge_rejected` — why merge was rejected (invalid proof, overlapping ranges, etc.)
   - `mvcc_policy_switched` — from/to policy, trigger reason, expected-loss delta
   - `mvcc_contention_sample` — periodic contention summary (merge success rate, unnecessary abort rate)

2. **Evidence integration** — New preset `ffs evidence --preset contention` showing:
   - Merge success rate over time
   - Top conflict patterns (which block ranges conflict most)
   - Policy switching history

3. **Log contract tests** — For each marker: emit under correct condition, correct fields present, parseable.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 5 merge-specific log markers emitted in correct scenarios
- Evidence preset for contention data returns structured JSON
- Log contract tests for each marker
- E2E script validating all markers under concurrent merge workload
- Unit tests for contention sample aggregation

## Non-goals
- Generic MVCC metrics (conflict_rate, abort_rate, commit_rate) — those are in bd-m5wf.8.3","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:11.796076374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:07:26.133871015Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["logging","mvcc","observability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:11.796076374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:06.431902315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
